name: The Untended Antiquity
statement:
Adieu l'ami.
Koyomi is helping Oshino, an acquaintance of his, to take care of an open space around the abandoned Eikou Cram School building, Oshino's makeshift residence.
The space is represented by a rectangular grid of n‚ÄČ√ó‚ÄČm cells, arranged into n rows and m columns. The c-th cell in the r-th row is denoted by (r,‚ÄČc).
Oshino places and removes barriers around rectangular areas of cells. Specifically, an action denoted by "1 r1 c1 r2 c2" means Oshino's placing barriers around a rectangle with two corners being (r1,‚ÄČc1) and (r2,‚ÄČc2) and sides parallel to squares sides. Similarly, "2 r1 c1 r2 c2" means Oshino's removing barriers around the rectangle. Oshino ensures that no barriers staying on the ground share any common points, nor do they intersect with boundaries of the n‚ÄČ√ó‚ÄČm area.
Sometimes Koyomi tries to walk from one cell to another carefully without striding over barriers, in order to avoid damaging various items on the ground. "3 r1 c1 r2 c2" means that Koyomi tries to walk from (r1,‚ÄČc1) to (r2,‚ÄČc2) without crossing barriers.
And you're here to tell Koyomi the feasibility of each of his attempts.
input:
The first line of input contains three space-separated integers n, m and q (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn,‚ÄČm‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ2‚ÄČ500, 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČq‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ100‚ÄČ000) ‚ÄĒ the number of rows and columns in the grid, and the total number of Oshino and Koyomi's actions, respectively.
The following q lines each describes an action, containing five space-separated integers t, r1, c1, r2, c2 (1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČt‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČ3, 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČr1,‚ÄČr2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn, 1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČc1,‚ÄČc2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČm) ‚ÄĒ the type and two coordinates of an action. Additionally, the following holds depending on the value of t:
‚ÄĘ  If t‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ1: 2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČr1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČr2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn‚ÄČ-‚ÄČ1, 2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČc1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČc2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČm‚ÄČ-‚ÄČ1;
‚ÄĘ  If t‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ2: 2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČr1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČr2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČn‚ÄČ-‚ÄČ1, 2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČc1‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČc2‚ÄČ‚Č§‚ÄČm‚ÄČ-‚ÄČ1, the specified group of barriers exist on the ground before the removal.
‚ÄĘ  If t‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ3: no extra restrictions.
output: For each of Koyomi's attempts (actions with t‚ÄČ=‚ÄČ3), output one line ‚ÄĒ containing "Yes" (without quotes) if it's feasible, and "No" (without quotes) otherwise.
note: For the first example, the situations of Koyomi's actions are illustrated below.
